hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) residues, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E, D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic interactions without compromising SSTR2 binding. This will increase the selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: disrupting off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the FWKT(7-10) motif and Cys3/Cys14 is the key to achieving SSTR2 selectivity. However, structural failures are limiting the number of viable candidates.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the allowed clash score will enforce better packing and reduce structural failures.
Increasing the number of candidates will improve diversity and increase the chances of generating structurally valid designs.
